title: SACRAMENTO, CA
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 3042 seeks to establish a permanent source of non-General Fund revenue to support DNA programs aimed at solving violent crimes and exonerating the innocent.
For nearly 20 years, the DNA Fingerprint, Unsolved Crime, and Innocence Protection Act has assisted law enforcement in solving cold cases, including heinous crimes of rape and murder.
The state's DNA database program has been instrumental in solving crimes and exonerating wrongfully accused individuals.
AB 3042 will provide a stable source of revenue outside the General Fund to support DNA testing programs at the local and state levels.
The Missing Persons DNA Program is one of the essential services provided by the Bureau of Forensic Services.
The Bureau of Forensic Services receives crucial funding through Proposition 69.
AB 3042 would ensure that Proposition 69 remains in place to support efforts to solve crime through forensic services.
